message:
Last month our power went out for about 6 hours, and I learned something humbling: my kids were WAY more prepared than I was. 

While I was shuffling around looking for a flashlight (which I couldn't find), my 9-year-old had already gathered blankets, pulled out a battery-powered lantern from her emergency kit, AND made sure everyone's phones were on low-power mode. My teenager? Already thinking about snacks that don't need the fridge.

I was the adult supposedly "in charge," and I felt like I was playing catch-up to their calm logic. Turns out, when you actually talk through what happens during a blackout—and maybe even do a little practice run together—kids totally get it. They're not scared; they're just... ready.

It made me realize: emergency prep isn't about being paranoid. It's about being the person your family can actually lean on, not the one frantically searching for a working flashlight in the dark.

Do your kids know where your flashlights are? (And more importantly, do *you*? 😄)
